name: RomCom: Unzipping Trouble
description:
Scenario Overview

The SOC dashboard just lit up with a series of critical alerts originating from a corporate endpoint. What started as a standard phishing alert for an email attachment named Project_Q3_Requirements.rar quickly escalated into a full-blown domain compromise.

Initial incident response triage indicates that the user extracted the archive, inadvertently triggering a directory traversal vulnerability (CVE-2025-8088 / CVE-2025-6218). This allowed the attacker to silently drop a VBS script directly into the user's Startup folder, achieving persistence.

From there, the threat actor wasted no time. The logs show PowerShell downloading a suspicious DLL, Rundll32 execution leading to C2 beaconing, LSASS memory dumping via ProcDump, and eventual lateral movement to the Domain Controller using WMI.

Threat Intelligence suggests this TTP profile aligns with the RomCom threat group, utilizing the RustyClaw/Mythic agent.

Your Mission

As the lead SOC Analyst, your task is to analyze the provided JSON alert logs and reconstruct the attacker's entire kill chain. You will need to:

  • Identify the C2 infrastructure and malicious payloads.
  • Trace the discovery and credential dumping techniques.
  • Determine exactly how the attacker compromised the Domain Controller.

Can you untangle this malicious affair and secure the network?
